articles.title: U.S. oil tops $90, Brent above $98 after tankers struck off Saudi Arabia
articles.description: Oil prices rose after attacks on ships in the Red Sea, and U.S. President Donald Trump's threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure.

articles.title: MORNING GLORY: President Trump, Iran and Napoleon’s still-relevant maxim
articles.description: Marco Rubio calls Iran's leaders crazy as Trump faces pressure to finish the battle against a regime driven by end-times theology and nuclear aims.

related.title: How Trump Is Getting Richer as President
related.description: Since he came back to office, President Trump has made huge amounts of money from cryptocurrency and foreign investments, posing the question of whether he is using his power as president to enrich himself. Andrea Fuller, a Times data journalist, explains.
related.normalizedTitle: how trump is getting richer as president

articles.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
articles.description: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.

articles.title: Global Oil Price Nears $100 a Barrel as Conflict With Iran Deepens
articles.description: The Houthis claimed to have attacked two ships in the Red Sea, and Iran said a third caught fire near the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: SEN RAND PAUL: DC's Russia sanctions bill won't stop Putin — but it will hurt you
articles.description: This new Russia sanctions bill could impose 500% tariffs and cost Americans half a trillion dollars, targeting 40% of the world's population.

articles.title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
articles.description: At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.

related.title: Google slapped with $1 billion fine under landmark EU digital law
related.description: European regulators fined Google 890 million euros ($1 billion) alleging the company gives preferential treatment to its own services.
related.normalizedTitle: google slapped with 1 billion fine under landmark eu digital law

articles.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Global Nuclear Arms Race
articles.description: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.

articles.title: Trump launches Gold Eagle to hunt cyber flaws with AI
articles.description: The White House Gold Eagle program uses Anthropic's Claude Mythos to hunt software vulnerabilities, but major questions remain about oversight and access.

articles.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
articles.description: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.

articles.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
articles.description: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

articles.title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
articles.description: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”

articles.title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
articles.description: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.

articles.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
articles.description: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

articles.title: Russia’s Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries is now in Ukraine’s crosshairs. Here's why
articles.description: Ukraine has ratcheted up its attacks on Russia's Amazon-style retail giant Wildberries, signaling a new phase in the war.

articles.title: House Adopts $95 Billion G.O.P. Budget for Iran War
articles.description: Republican leaders muscled through resistance in their own ranks to approve the budget blueprint for the war and other measures, but its prospects were far dimmer in the Senate.

articles.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
articles.description: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

articles.title: Alaska’s Dan Sullivan tells 'Ruthless' he will overcome Dem effort to ‘steal’ his name
articles.description: Alaska Sen. Dan Sullivan sits down with the "Ruthless Podcast" to discuss his critical re-election fight that could determine control of the U.S. Senate.

articles.title: Houthis claim strikes on Saudi oil tankers as Rubio says Iran not ready to make a deal
articles.description: Yemen's Houthis said they had targeted two Saudi oil tankers with drones and missiles for allegedly violating its maritime blockade.

articles.title: Senate panel advances China auto bill that could bar Mercedes-Benz from U.S.
articles.description: Mercedes-Benz’s largest individual shareholder is Chinese state-owned BAIC. Cruz warned the bill could hurt Mercedes and accused GM of backing it.

articles.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano may have misled Congress, Democrats say
articles.description: The IRS alone hemorrhaged more than 31,000 employees as of January in President Trump's government cuts, according to the agency's inspector general.

articles.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
articles.description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

articles.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
articles.description: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

articles.title: AOC roasted over defense bill claim as GOP lawmaker tells her to 'stop stoking Jew hatred'
articles.description: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez claims the NDAA would merge parts of the U.S. military with the Israel Defense Forces, drawing sharp pushback from Republicans.

articles.title: How the Pentagon Withholds Iran War Details
articles.description: Our Pentagon correspondent Helene Cooper explains how a lack of transparency reflects a tension between the Pentagon’s claims of operational security and the American public’s right to know what the military is doing in Iran.

articles.title: Photographic Evidence
articles.description: We look at how The Times uses photographs and video to better understand what’s happening in the war with Iran.

articles.title: Veterans fought for these benefits. Congress can finally give them what they need
articles.description: Combat-injured veterans and 14.3 million military caregivers await action on a bill addressing disability compensation, survivor benefits and more.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia’s Nuclear Plans
articles.description: The U.S. will help the Saudis develop a civilian nuclear program, with uncertain consequences for the Middle East.

articles.title: Nicaragua's parliament plans to eliminate elections to codify President Ortega's declaration
articles.description: Nicaragua's congress moves to abolish elections after President Daniel Ortega declared no more elections, drawing condemnation from the international community.

articles.title: Massachusetts House Passes Bill That Would Loosen Late-Term Abortion Rules
articles.description: The bill would leave it to physicians to decide if an abortion is necessary after 24 weeks of pregnancy. Current law allows such abortions only under certain circumstances.

articles.title: Google Sets the Pace on Hefty A.I. Spending
articles.description: The technology giant again raised its projections for investing in artificial intelligence. Many on Wall Street still worry about the eventual payoff.

articles.title: U.S. Employers Forced to Fire Thousands of Immigrants, and a Showdown over Peptides
articles.description: Plus, America says no thanks to salad.

articles.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
articles.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.

articles.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
articles.description: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

articles.title: Should Travelers Give Up on Summer in Western Europe Because of the Heat?
articles.description: A favorite vacation option may be at risk because of rising temperatures. Even those sticking with their summer plans agree: More air conditioning would help.

articles.title: Gunman Faces Prison Sentence for Shooting Minnesota Lawmakers
articles.description: The Minnesota man who attacked state lawmakers in their homes last year was expected to be sentenced to life in prison.

articles.title: Gen Z Has Lost Its Ambition. Maybe It’s Found Something Better.
articles.description: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.

articles.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
articles.description: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.

articles.title: UniCredit CEO tells CNBC acquisition of Commerzbank could happen in fourth quarter
articles.description: UniCredit CEO Andrea Orcel told CNBC Thursday that the Italian bank could "potentially" acquire Commerzbank in the fourth quarter.

articles.title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
articles.description: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.

articles.title: Why I Sought Out a Taliban Commander I Had Fought
articles.description: First as a Marine and then as a journalist, I spent years tied to Afghanistan. I returned one last time to speak to a former enemy in search of answers, and perhaps closure.

articles.title: American Airlines slashes 2026 earnings outlook as fuel costs spike
articles.description: American Airlines further cut its 2026 earnings outlook, citing higher fuel costs.

articles.title: Sean Penn reveals chilling thought after finding himself alone with the 'Night Stalker' in jail
articles.description: Sean Penn reveals he was shackled just feet from serial killer Richard Ramirez in Los Angeles County Jail and briefly considered attacking the "Night Stalker."

articles.title: Archaeologists uncover 1,700-year-old Christian city hidden beneath Egypt's desert
articles.description: A fourth-century basilica church and archive of 200 written documents emerged from excavations by archaeologists at the Ain al-Sabil site in Egypt's Dakhla Oasis.

articles.title: Ozempic face? Botox boom boosted by 'sagging face' trend, Swiss firm says
articles.description: The aestetics company hope increased GLP-1 use will fuel demand for treatments addressing facial volume loss, sometimes dubbed "Ozempic face."

articles.title: Protesters gather in Madison after man fatally shot by a police officer
articles.description: A graphic video of the shooting during an attempted arrest in the Marquette neighborhood of Madison, Wisconsin, was quickly spread online.
